title: MOVE
description: Moves a key to another database.

returns.description: 1 if key was moved. 0 if key was not moved.

jedis.signature: long move(final byte[] key, final int dbIndex)

returns.description: 1 if key was moved. 0 if key was not moved.

jedis.signature: long move(final String key, final int dbIndex)

go-redis.signature: Move(ctx context.Context, key string, db int)

returns.description: Boolean integer-reply specifically: true if key was moved. false if key was not moved.
Boolean integer-reply specifically: true if key was moved. false if key was not moved.
Boolean integer-reply specifically: true if key was moved. false if key was not moved.

nredisstack_sync.signature: KeyMove(RedisKey key, int database, CommandFlags flags = CommandFlags.None)

params.description: The flags to use for this operation.

returns.description: whether the key was moved or not

content:
Move `key` from the currently selected database (see [`SELECT`]()) to the specified destination database. When `key` already exists in the destination database, or it does not exist in the source database, it does nothing. It is possible to use `MOVE` as a locking primitive because of this. ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ❌ Standard
❌ Active-Active | ❌ Standard
❌ Active-Active | Redis Software does not support shared databases due to potential negative performance impacts and blocks any related commands. | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="move-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if _key_ was moved. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _key_ wasn't moved. -tab-sep- One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if _key_ was moved. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _key_ wasn't moved. {{< /multitabs >}}
intro:
Move `key` from the currently selected database (see [`SELECT`]()) to the specified
destination database.
When `key` already exists in the destination database, or it does not exist in
the source database, it does nothing.
It is possible to use `MOVE` as a locking primitive because of this.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Move `key` from the currently selected database (see [`SELECT`]()) to the specified
destination database.
When `key` already exists in the destination database, or it does not exist in
the source database, it does nothing.
It is possible to use `MOVE` as a locking primitive because of this.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ❌ Standard
❌ Active-Active | ❌ Standard
❌ Active-Active | Redis Software does not support shared databases due to potential negative performance impacts and blocks any related commands. |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="move-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if _key_ was moved.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _key_ wasn't moved.

-tab-sep-

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if _key_ was moved.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _key_ wasn't moved.

{{< /multitabs >}}
